title: Protein/assay set (Western blot, immunofluorescence, viability assay)
context: Western blotting, immunofluorescence, and cell viability assays are among the most cited and most variably performed methods in cancer biology, and are a documented source of irreproducible results. These protocols involve acrylamide (neurotoxin), methanol, formaldehyde (potential carcinogen), and fluorescent reagents, all requiring careful hazard documentation. This task delivers the protein/assay subset of the M2 seed library through the full validated pipeline.
objective: Author, validate, lint, and ship a set of protein and assay protocols (Western blot, immunofluorescence, cell viability assay) through the full lab-protocols-open pipeline, each schema-valid, linter-green, with 100% provenance, complete safety sections, and credentialed safety sign-off.
acceptanceCriteria: At least three protocols authored: Western blot (gel electrophoresis + transfer + detection), immunofluorescence staining, and a cell viability assay (e.g., MTT or equivalent open-licensed method); each schema-valid and linter-green.
Hazardous reagents documented per step: acrylamide (neurotoxin/carcinogen), methanol, formaldehyde, and fluorescent reagents with SDS refs; credentialed safety sign-off recorded.
100% of steps and safety claims carry resolvable, license-verified provenance references.
License+Ethics gate PASS per protocol; dual-use screen clear; all mandatory disclaimers present.
semver v1.0.0 + CHANGELOG per protocol; rendered Markdown/HTML produced; CI green; commits DCO signed-off.
resources: ECHA/GHS SDS sources for acrylamide, methanol, formaldehyde
protocols.io (per-protocol CC-BY source candidates — license must be verified per item)
output: Committed, fully-reviewed protein/assay protocol records (Western blot, immunofluorescence, viability assay) with rendered outputs, license evidence, and all review sign-offs; CI green; commits DCO signed-off.
